na.summary: The GENIUS Act is reshaping U.S. stablecoin regulation on multiple fronts: FinCEN and OFAC proposed a rule requiring payment stablecoin issuers to implement GENIUS Act compliance measures, while the FDIC separately proposed its own GENIUS Act rules that are being compared to an OCC proposal. The GENIUS Act and STABLE Act reached a critical juncture by late April 2026, with banks losing their last opportunity to shape the regulatory framework. On the institutional side, Morgan Stanley became a stablecoin reserve manager, and Western Union announced it is moving toward becoming a stablecoin issuer rather than a user of the technology. Circle, facing a lawsuit over a $280 million Drift Protocol hack, also announced plans to offer 10 million shares. Separately, the world's largest stablecoin issuer froze a record $344 million on the Tron network, underscoring the scale of compliance activity now running through the emerging regulatory infrastructure.

highlights.text: FinCEN and OFAC proposed a rule requiring payment stablecoin issuers to implement GENIUS Act compliance measures
FDIC separately proposed its own GENIUS Act rules
GENIUS Act and STABLE Act reached a critical juncture
Morgan Stanley became a stablecoin reserve manager
Western Union announced it is moving toward becoming a stablecoin issuer
froze a record $344 million on the Tron network
lawsuit over a $280 million Drift Protocol hack

eu.summary: The UK Financial Conduct Authority is conducting a consultation on cryptoasset activities including stablecoin issuance, with full regulatory requirements expected to be implemented in 2026, creating new compliance obligations for UK payment firms. Bank of England Governor Andrew Bailey has flagged that progress on global stablecoin standards has slowed, adding urgency to domestic rulemaking as Coinbase listed the first GBP stablecoin on April 22. In Switzerland, UBS joined five other Swiss banks to launch a sandbox initiative testing a Swiss franc stablecoin designed to bridge blockchain technology with traditional fiat payment systems. Worldline has partnered with Circle to integrate stablecoin payment capabilities into its managed payments offering, signaling growing private-sector momentum alongside these regulatory developments.

highlights.text: compliance obligations for UK payment firms
progress on global stablecoin standards has slowed
Coinbase listed the first GBP stablecoin
UBS joined five other Swiss banks to launch a sandbox initiative
bridge blockchain technology with traditional fiat payment systems
Worldline has partnered with Circle

asia.summary: Hong Kong granted its first stablecoin licenses to HSBC and Standard Chartered under its regulatory framework, but the rollout has been shadowed by fraud: the Hong Kong Monetary Authority has warned the public about counterfeit tokens impersonating HSBC and HKDAP ahead of any licensed products reaching the market. In Japan, the Financial Services Agency classified JPYC under its regulated payment services framework, coinciding with JPYC surpassing $100 million in circulation supported by Polygon. Australia released a draft payments vision that includes consideration of stablecoin interoperability, advancing reforms to its payment infrastructure as part of a broader overhaul. Across the region, stablecoin adoption in Asia Pacific has moved beyond experimental deployments, with Western Union planning a B2B stablecoin launch for agent settlements in May 2026 and Visa expanding its stablecoin settlement pilot to include five additional blockchains.

highlights.text: first stablecoin licenses to HSBC and Standard Chartered
Hong Kong Monetary Authority has warned the public about counterfeit tokens impersonating HSBC and HKDAP
Financial Services Agency classified JPYC under its regulated payment services framework
JPYC surpassing $100 million in circulation
draft payments vision that includes consideration of stablecoin interoperability
stablecoin adoption in Asia Pacific has moved beyond experimental deployments
Western Union planning a B2B stablecoin launch for agent settlements in May 2026
Visa expanding its stablecoin settlement pilot to include five additional blockchains
